concepts.lesson: The pH scale is a measure of the concentration of hydrogen ions in a solution, ranging from 0 to 14. A pH of 7 is neutral, while values below 7 are acidic and above 7 are basic.
Chemical bonding occurs when two or more atoms share electrons to form a molecule. There are different types of bonds, including covalent and ionic bonds.
key_points: Atoms share electrons in covalent bonding

concepts.lesson: The alkali metals are a group of elements in the periodic table that are highly reactive. They include lithium, sodium, and potassium.
pH indicators are substances that change color in response to changes in pH. They can be used to measure the acidity or basicity of a solution.
Acid-base equilibrium occurs when a weak acid and a strong base react to form a salt and water. This process can be represented by an equation.
key_points: Weak acid + Strong base → Salt + Water

concepts.lesson: Electron configuration is a way of describing the arrangement of electrons in an atom. It can be used to predict the properties and behavior of elements.
